title: System and method for photonic digital to analog conversion
abstract: An n-bit electro-optic modulator (EOM) (300) for converting an electrical signal into an optical signal is provided. The EOM (300) includes a plurality of n optical modulators (313 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ), pairs of the plurality of n optical modulators (313 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ),each receiving light at a common wavelength of n/2 different wavelengths ( 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n/2 ), and each of the n optical modulators (313 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ) amplitude encoding one of n bits of an input digital n-bit source (215) using the corresponding received common wavelength of light according to a significance of that digital bit. A plurality of n/2 beam combiners (315 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n/2 ) are provided. Each of the beam combiners (315 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n/2 ) receives a pair of modulated outputs (317 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ) from one of the pairs of optical modulators (313 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ) that received the light at common wavelength and combines the modulated outputs (317 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n ) pair to produce a single dual-polarized output (318 1 Ă˘Â€Â¦Ă˘Â€Â¦n/2 ). A system and a method are provided for converting an electrical signal into an optical signal. Transmission and reception methods are provided for transmitting and receiving a radio-over-fiber signal.
